candidate_tasks.title: roadmap.md sync for P246–P248A arc
candidate_tasks.value: Medium — governance completeness; prevents future agents from treating P246-P248A as open
candidate_tasks.risk: Very low — doc-only, no code/DB change
candidate_tasks.urgency: Low-medium — stale roadmap creates governance drift risk
candidate_tasks.reason: roadmap.md last updated P213L (2026-06-05) and does not reflect the P246B–P248A arc. A Type B doc-only sync recording the BIG_LOTTO canonical isolation closure into the roadmap phase table is low-risk, natural, and prevents future agent confusion. No authorization beyond Type B governance templates required.

candidate_tasks.title: CURRENT_STATE.md row-count label clarification
candidate_tasks.value: Low-medium — governance accuracy; 'BIG_LOTTO rows | 24,140' is replay rows not draw rows
candidate_tasks.recommended_now: YES (can be combined with T1 in same PR)
candidate_tasks.reason: CURRENT_STATE.md shows 'BIG_LOTTO rows | 24,140'. This is actually replay rows (24,140 + 34,680 + 36,104 = 94,924 total replay rows), not draw rows. Actual BIG_LOTTO draw rows = 22,238. Clarifying the label or adding a note prevents future agents from misinterpreting this as the BIG_LOTTO draw count. Combined with T1 this is a single Type B PR.

candidate_tasks.title: Archived BIG_LOTTO script migration (deferred)
candidate_tasks.value: Low — scripts not in active pipeline
candidate_tasks.urgency: Very low — only needed when scripts are reactivated
candidate_tasks.reason: P247F/P247D documented ~20 archived lottery_api/backtest_*.py and predict_*.py scripts still using get_all_draws('BIG_LOTTO'). These are deferred. Migration should happen when/if a specific script is reactivated for use, not proactively for dormant code.
candidate_tasks.authorization_required: None for code, but user activation decision needed first

candidate_tasks.title: Annotation table Type D planning/apply
candidate_tasks.value: Low — canonical isolation already complete via DB view; annotation adds row-family labels
candidate_tasks.risk: Low-medium — controlled DB write; backup required
candidate_tasks.urgency: Very low — no active consumer needs it
candidate_tasks.reason: draw_row_family_annotations was designed in P247A as optional Phase 2. P247B/C/D/E/F/G completed BIG_LOTTO isolation using only the view. No active research path requires annotation labels. Deferring until a specific consumer use-case requires per-row family labels.
candidate_tasks.authorization_required: Explicit Type D: YES apply P249D CREATE TABLE draw_row_family_annotations

candidate_tasks.title: DAILY_539 new hypothesis pre-registration design
candidate_tasks.risk: Low if read-only; medium if leads to OOS run
candidate_tasks.urgency: Low — all prior candidates rejected; no active WAIT_FOR_OOS
candidate_tasks.prerequisites: New hypothesis not from L82-L82 rejected list; P221F protocol gates; pre-registration required before any data-touching
candidate_tasks.recommended_now: NO (unless user has specific hypothesis to test)
candidate_tasks.reason: DAILY_539 midfreq_fourier_2bet rejected by P230C. No active WAIT_FOR_OOS. P224B gate condition (≥300 new draws) is irrelevant since the candidate was rejected, not merely deferred. A new pre-registered hypothesis is needed before any fresh scan. Not recommended without explicit user direction and a new hypothesis.

candidate_tasks.title: POWER_LOTTO new hypothesis pre-registration design
candidate_tasks.prerequisites: Significant new POWER_LOTTO draws; new hypothesis not from closed lines; P221F protocol
candidate_tasks.reason: P231B first-zone NULL. P222 found weak in-sample candidates for POWER_LOTTO but no cross-year confirmation. Any new direction requires explicit pre-registration and user authorization, not proactive discovery.
candidate_tasks.authorization_required: Explicit user direction + Type A pre-registration

candidate_tasks.title: 3_STAR/4_STAR per-position replay code build
candidate_tasks.type: code-change / Type D if DB write
candidate_tasks.value: Low-medium — positional data is in DB; P214C showed NULL
candidate_tasks.risk: Medium — significant code + DB write for replay generation
candidate_tasks.urgency: Very low — P214C: 0 Bonferroni-significant, NULL result
candidate_tasks.prerequisites: Explicit authorization; fresh pre-registration for any new scan
candidate_tasks.reason: 3_STAR/4_STAR positional data ingested (P213H/P213L). Straight-play code designed (P213D-E). P214C Bonferroni scan showed 0 significant findings (NULL result). Building replay infrastructure for a NULL signal has low return on investment. Only recommended if user has a specific new pre-registered positional hypothesis.

candidate_tasks.title: Raw history UI/API add-on labeling
candidate_tasks.value: Low — no active consumer requesting it
candidate_tasks.risk: Medium — requires frontend + API behavior change (outside P249A scope)
candidate_tasks.reason: ADD_ON_PRIZE_EXCLUDED records are raw-accessible. No current frontend/API consumer is requesting row-family labels. This is a future UX task if users need to distinguish add-on vs canonical draws in the history display.

recommended_next_task.title: roadmap.md sync + CURRENT_STATE.md row-count label clarification
recommended_next_task.rationale: Both are zero-risk, Type B doc-only governance cleanup tasks that can be done in a single PR. T1 records the completed P246B–P248A arc in roadmap.md. T2 clarifies that 'BIG_LOTTO rows | 24,140' in CURRENT_STATE.md refers to replay rows, not draw rows (actual BIG_LOTTO draw rows = 22,238). No DB write. No code change. No strategy promotion. No authorization required beyond Type B.

final_decision: P249A triage complete. P246–P248A closure verified. 8 candidate tasks ranked; T1+T2 recommended (Type B doc-only: roadmap.md sync + CURRENT_STATE label fix). All major research lines closed or NULL. No active WAIT_FOR_OOS. No DB write. No prediction/betting recommendation.
